nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Parse option-attached paths before allowlist checks**

Path enforcement is skipped whenever `parsePathToken` returns `null`, so tokens like `--git-dir=/etc` or `--work-tree=/tmp` are treated as non-path args and execute instead of being blocked. I confirmed from `git -h` that these are valid path-bearing options (`[--git-dir=<path>] [--work-tree=<path>]`), so the current check allows access outside allowlisted roots via option-value syntax.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Resolve candidate paths before root containment checks**

The root check resolves each allowed root with `realpathSync` but compares it against the unresolved candidate path, which lets symlink paths pass containment even when they point outside allowlisted roots. In practice, a symlink under `engine/` can target `/tmp` (or elsewhere) and `node engine/link.js` is accepted, bypassing the path sandbox.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Return blocked receipt instead of throwing on invalid cwd**

Validation calls `resolvePathWithinRoots` for `cwd`/`repoRoot`, but that helper throws on out-of-scope paths; because `executeSandboxedCommand` does not catch this, the API throws and no blocked receipt is written. This breaks the function’s receipt contract for exactly the policy-violation cases that should be reported as controlled denials.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
